title: ScientificTalkExtraction_v2
description: Per-speaker extraction of oncology/pharmacology research presented at AACR 2026. One record per individual speaker talk within a session. DO NOT create a record for session chairs who only give introductory remarks with no primary data — skip them. DO create a record for every speaker who presents original data, a clinical trial, or a substantive scientific argument.

talk_id.description: Constructed as '{session_slug}::{speaker_last_name}::{talk_index_within_session}'. e.g. 'cancer-vaccines::wu::2'. Index starts at 1 for the first data-presenting speaker.

talk_title.description: Title of this specific talk as stated by the speaker or chair. If not explicitly stated, construct a concise descriptive title from the content (max 12 words). Do NOT leave blank.

name.description: Full name of the speaker as introduced or self-identified.

affiliation.description: Institution or company. Include department if stated.

role.description: Role of the speaker. Use 'session_chair_with_data' only if the chair also presents substantive data or a scientific argument.

disclosures_noted.description: True if the speaker explicitly mentioned financial disclosures (even if none).

tumor_types.description: Cancer types discussed. Use standard names: 'colorectal cancer', 'NSCLC', 'pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma', 'melanoma', 'AML', etc. Use 'pan-cancer' if applicable. Use [] if no specific cancer type is discussed (e.g. pure methods talk).

clinical_stage.description: Most advanced clinical stage of the primary work presented. Use 'preclinical' for cell line / mouse model only work. Use 'unspecified' only if truly unclear.

topic_categories.description: High-level topic tags. Choose ALL that apply from: immunotherapy, cancer_vaccines, CAR_T, ADC, targeted_therapy, PROTAC_degrader, molecular_glue, epigenetics, DNA_damage_repair, cell_cycle, metabolism, tumor_microenvironment, spatial_biology, liquid_biopsy, ctDNA, biomarker_development, early_detection, microbiome, synthetic_biology, radiopharmaceuticals, combination_therapy, resistance_mechanisms, clinical_trial, translational, basic_science, platform_technology, AI_ML, other.

targets.description: Biological targets discussed. One entry per distinct target/drug. Use [] if no specific molecular target is discussed (e.g. a talk purely about imaging methods). For each target, fill as many fields as the transcript supports.

gene_or_protein.description: Standard gene symbol or protein name. e.g. 'KRAS', 'PD-L1', 'HER2', 'TROP2'.

alteration.description: Specific mutation or alteration if relevant. e.g. 'G12C', 'G12D', 'amplification', 'fusion'. Omit if not applicable.

pathway.description: Signaling pathway. e.g. 'RAS/MAPK', 'PI3K/AKT', 'WNT', 'DNA_damage_response', 'immune_checkpoint', 'cell_surface_antigen'. Use 'unknown' if not stated.

modality.description: Therapeutic modality targeting this gene/protein. Use 'unspecified' only if truly unclear.

compound_name.description: Drug name (generic, brand, or internal code). e.g. 'sotorasib', 'AMG 510', 'mRNA-4157', 'SynthBody X8'. Omit if no compound named.

compound_id.description: NCT number, ClinicalTrials ID, or internal code if mentioned. Omit if not stated.

ligase_or_cofactor.description: For degraders/molecular glues only: E3 ligase recruited. e.g. 'CRBN', 'VHL', 'MDM2'. Omit if not applicable.

dependency_context.description: Genetic/molecular context where the dependency or activity exists. e.g. 'KRAS G12C mutant NSCLC', 'HER2-amplified breast cancer', 'MMR-deficient CRC', 'all solid tumors'. Omit if not specified.

target_novelty.description: How established is this target in the oncology field.

biomarkers.description: Patient selection, predictive, prognostic, or pharmacodynamic biomarkers discussed. Use [] if none discussed.

name.description: Biomarker name. e.g. 'MSI-H', 'TMB-high', 'ctDNA clearance', 'PD-L1 CPS', 'KRAS G12C', 'CD8 TIL density'.

assay.description: How it was measured. e.g. 'IHC', 'NGS panel', 'WGS', 'ctDNA liquid biopsy (Next Personal)', 'IHC CPS score', 'ELISpot'. Omit if not stated.

finding.description: Key finding about this biomarker. Be specific and include numbers. e.g. 'ctDNA clearance at week 6 predicted pCR (sensitivity 89%, specificity 100%)'.

models.description: Experimental models used. For clinical talks, the patient cohort IS the model — use model_type 'clinical_cohort' or 'clinical_trial'. Use [] only if no experimental work described at all.

name.description: Specific model name. e.g. 'SW480', 'KPC mouse', 'PANC-1 PDX', 'NeoPRISM-CRC trial', 'TCGA cohort'. Use 'not specified' if model type is clear but name is not given.

genotype_or_key_features.description: Relevant genotype, features, or cohort characteristics. e.g. 'KRAS G12D/+; Trp53 R172H/+', 'MMRd stage II-III CRC, n=32', 'HER2-high breast cancer, n=127 evaluable'. Use 'not specified' if not stated.

clinical_data.description: ALL quantitative clinical efficacy or safety data reported. Extract every number. Use [] for purely preclinical talks.

value.description: Reported value with units. e.g. '59%', '14.2 months', 'HR 0.56', 'p=0.003', 'not reached'.

confidence_interval.description: 95% CI if reported. e.g. '95% CI 0.38-0.82'. Omit if not reported.

n.description: Sample size for this datapoint. e.g. 'n=32', 'n=127 evaluable', 'n=25 with ctDNA data'.

population.description: Patient population this applies to. e.g. 'MMRd CRC stage II-III', 'all-comers', 'ctDNA-positive at baseline', 'pCR subgroup'.

comparator.description: Comparator arm if applicable. e.g. 'vs pembrolizumab alone', 'vs historical control 30%'. Omit if single-arm.

MOA_summary.description: 2-4 sentence mechanistic summary of the primary scientific contribution. Be specific — name the pathway, the intervention, and the observed effect. For clinical talks, summarize the clinical hypothesis and key result. Do NOT write generic summaries like 'the speaker discussed cancer immunotherapy'.

readouts.description: All assay types and readouts used. e.g. 'RNA-seq', 'scRNA-seq', 'Visium_spatial_transcriptomics', 'ELISpot', 'flow_cytometry', 'IHC', 'WGS', 'ctDNA_WGS', 'western_blot', 'CRISPR_screen', 'proteomics', 'CyTOF', 'TCR_sequencing', 'multiplex_IHC', 'ELISA'. Use [] if no assays described.

key_findings.description: 3-7 bullet points of the major scientific discoveries or clinical results. RULES: (1) Each bullet must be a complete, specific, data-grounded statement. (2) Include numbers where reported. (3) Do NOT use vague language like 'results were promising' or 'showed improvement'. (4) Bad example: 'The vaccine showed clinical activity.' Good example: 'Adjuvant mRNA neoantigen vaccine + pembrolizumab improved RFS vs pembrolizumab alone (HR 0.56, 95% CI 0.38-0.82) in resected high-risk melanoma (n=157, Phase IIb randomized)'.

novelty_flag.description: Scientific novelty classification of the primary contribution. 'clinical_validation_of_known' = confirming known biology in patients. 'negative_or_null_result' = primary endpoint missed or hypothesis disproven.

resistance_or_selectivity_notes.description: Specific observations about resistance mechanisms, off-target effects, paralog redundancy, narrow therapeutic windows, or selectivity challenges. Quote or closely paraphrase the speaker. Use [] if none discussed.

combination_strategies.description: Drug combinations tested or proposed. Use [] if none discussed.

with_agent.description: Name of the combination partner drug or modality.

rationale.description: Scientific rationale for the combination as stated or implied by the speaker.

phenotype.description: Observed or predicted outcome with specifics. e.g. 'synergistic tumor regression in KPC mice (p<0.01)', 'improved pCR 59% vs 30% historical'.

stage.description: How far along is this combination.

open_questions.description: Explicit scientific or clinical questions the speaker raised but did not answer. High-value for identifying research gaps and follow-up directions. Use [] if none raised.

external_follow_up.description: Pointers for downstream enrichment agents. Populate only with what was explicitly mentioned in the transcript.

nct_numbers.description: ClinicalTrials.gov NCT numbers mentioned verbatim. e.g. ['NCT04165772', 'NCT05194995'].

abstract_ids.description: AACR or other conference abstract IDs mentioned.

cited_papers.description: Papers cited by author/year/journal as mentioned in the talk. e.g. 'Weber et al. 2024 NEJM', 'Wu et al. 2023 Nature'. Only include if author + year or journal was stated.

company_or_sponsor.description: Pharmaceutical companies or sponsors mentioned in context of the work.

patent_or_ip_hints.description: Any mentions of patents, licensing, or IP that warrant patent database lookup.
